title: Odyssey: Constructing Verifiable Local Truth-Preserving Foundation Models
optimized_headline: Building Trustworthy AI: The Quest for Verifiable Local Foundation Models

raw_body:
arXiv:2606.27593v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce a categorical framework called ODYSSEY for constructing verifiable, local truth-preserving foundation models as compositions of foundries: building-block architectural components that specify a cover of local contexts, local representation families, restriction maps, gluing rules, obstruction policies, update obligations, and human-facing views. A foundry is an organized sheaf of knowledge that carries within it an argumentation component. Concrete foundries are built from generic foundries such as evidence/argument, operational decision, institutional/financial, market meaning, scientific challenge, research-program, assistant-build, and evaluation-harness foundries. Universal Foundry Learning (UFL) formalizes foundry construction as a composition of left and right Kan extensions, with left Kan extension rolling local artifacts into candidate foundries and right Kan extension enforcing the restriction, gluing, obstruction, and argumentation conditions required for promotion. Foundry SQL (FSQL) is a small typed query surface for slicing maintained foundry artifacts that uses TICKET (Topos Integration using Causal Kan Extension Transformers) certification for admitting external or pre-built models into durable ODYSSEY state. ODYSSEY is fully implemented and tested across a wide spectrum of concrete foundries, showing that the same categorical machinery supports domain construction, artifact replay, sheaf diagnostics, grounded Toulmin/local-LLM scrutiny, residual-obstruction ledgers, and optimized TICKET-compatible causal-claim extraction across heterogeneous sources. This paper is to be presented as a 2.5 hour tutorial at ICML 2026. The tutorial home page is at https://bit.ly/4ajS0nA.
speedrun: The paper introduces ODYSSEY, a framework for creating verifiable, local truth-preserving foundation models. It utilizes building blocks called foundries, which organize knowledge and include components for argumentation and context management. Notably, it employs Universal Foundry Learning (UFL) to formalize foundry construction and Foundry SQL (FSQL) for querying. This development is significant as it aims to enhance the reliability of AI systems by ensuring they reflect localized truths effectively.
why_it_matters: Researchers and developers now have a structured approach to create AI models that prioritize accuracy and context, improving trust in AI outputs.
This framework could signal a shift in AI development towards more transparent and accountable models, potentially influencing market standards and practices.

lenses.eli12: ODYSSEY is like building a puzzle where each piece represents a part of knowledge. By ensuring the pieces fit together accurately, it helps create a clearer picture of truth in AI. This matters because it could lead to AI systems that are more reliable and trustworthy for everyone, from students to professionals.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, ODYSSEY offers a way to build AI that is not only efficient but also context-aware. This could reduce costs associated with misinformation and improve user trust. A practical implication is that products can be designed to adapt to local contexts, enhancing user experience and satisfaction.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, ODYSSEY employs advanced categorical methods like left and right Kan extensions to construct and validate foundries. It integrates TICKET certification for external model compatibility, ensuring robustness in various applications. This approach could improve diagnostics and argumentation in AI systems, though the complexity of the framework may present integration challenges.
